provenance.source_corpus: Not just any old pile of dirt: evaluating the use of artificial nesting mounds as conservation tools for freshwater turtles

source_spans.text: Four artificial nesting mounds were installed on 30 April 2009.
Hatching success was higher in nests transplanted to artificial mounds (93%) than in natural nests (56%)
indicating that artificial mounds have the potential to improve recruitment
Size-adjusted masses of C. picta hatchlings from nests on artificial mounds were not different from those of hatchlings from natural nests
deformity rates did not differ between treatments
Heat units accumulated during incubation did not differ between natural and artificial sites
Moisture levels were significantly lower in artificial mounds
time from oviposition to first emergence... did not differ between natural and artificial sites
